results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
🔥 Our Take: Your trackpad has had a haptic engine since 2015, and in ten years its only job has been faking the feel of a click. Breva is the first app we've seen use it for something human: the Mac physically breathes under your finger. Meditation apps live on your phone, the same device serving you the stress. Putting the two-minute reset inside the machine you work on, one keystroke from whatever spiked your heart rate, is the right placement. One commenter said it feels "like the mac itself is breathing with you." That's the whole product, and it's enough.
